name: Black Sesame Custard Bun
steps: Warm the milk to 37°C, add the yeast and sugar and carbon.
In the mixing bowl with the hook, mix the flour and the milk.
Once a dough has formed add the egg.
Add the cold butter to the dough a little bit at a time.
Once butter incorporated add the salt and mix on medium speed 5 minutes until dough is smooth.
Leave to rest, weigh out 30g balls.
Whisk together the sugar, yolks and corn flour.
Warm the milk and combine with yolk mixture.
Once cooked pour the mixture over the chocolate and emulsify, then add sesame paste and allow to cool in the fridge.
Once in pipe form, split into small spheres at 25g each.
Freeze and demould and store in freezer.
Cover the frozen custard with 60% dough from earlier.
Prove in the oven at 30°C for 30 minutes with 60% humidity.
Once proved, bake at 190°C and cook for 8 minutes.
Remove cool and store in the fridge.
